service.description:
Implements run#DeviceRequestInternal for the standalone moqui-device-gateway process. Called by run#DeviceRequest when DeviceRequest.runServiceName = "moqui.device.DeviceGatewayServices.run#GatewayDeviceRequest"
Data Model Conventions Each moqui-device-gateway instance is modelled as a Device in its own right:
Device.deviceId = moqui-device-gateway1 (one per gateway process) Device.deviceName = "Moqui Device Gateway 1"
The Moqui-side dispatch DeviceRequest (deviceId = gateway device) is a routing record only. It is distinct from the gateway-side DeviceRequest (different deviceId, e.g. virtual-plc-1) that the gateway reads from the shared DB to obtain fieldbus configuration (MQTT broker URI, OPC UA connection, topics, etc.).
Moqui-side DeviceRequest fields: deviceId = moqui-device-gateway1 routerEnumId = DrrMoquiDeviceGateway runServiceName = moqui.device.DeviceGatewayServices.run#GatewayDeviceRequest requestTypeEnumId = same type as the target gateway-side request (DrtWrite / DrtSubscribe / DrtUnsubscribe / etc.) brokerUri = gateway REST base URL e.g. http://gateway-host:8081 http://gateway-host:8081?apiKey=secret query = requestName of the gateway-side DeviceRequest e.g. VPL_MQTT_PUBLISH_REQ (deviceId=virtual-plc-1) connectionName = DeviceConnection (driverEnumId=DcdOpcUa) — required for OPC UA requests; the gateway reads the same record to resolve the OPC UA server address. Leave null for MQTT requests. timeout = HTTP timeout in seconds (optional; defaults to 30)
Gateway-side DeviceRequest fields (unchanged — fieldbus configuration): deviceId = virtual-plc-1 (the actual field device) requestName = VPL_MQTT_PUBLISH_REQ ← matches Moqui-side query field brokerUri = paho-mqtt5:virtual_plc_reference?brokerUrl=... (etc.)
Dispatch Mapping: every supported DeviceRequest is forwarded to the single gateway endpoint POST /api/device-request/run/{query} and the standalone gateway dispatches internally based on the gateway-side DeviceRequest.requestTypeEnumId and connection metadata.
Notes requestItemList and bulkRequestItemList are received but intentionally discarded: the gateway re-executes all DB queries autonomously using its own connection pool, so forwarding pre-resolved items would be redundant and potentially stale.
The onlyChangedParameters guard in run#DeviceRequest remains valuable: when no parameter has changed the gateway is never called at all.

Exports a device configuration recipe (Codesys txt-recipe format) via the moqui-device-gateway process.
The gateway queries DEVICE_RULE_SET → DEVICE_RULE → DEVICE_CONFIG → PARAMETER, groups rules by DEVICE_RULE.PRIORITY, and produces one recipe file per priority group.
File format (one line per parameter): DeviceName.ParameterName:=ParameterValue
Filename pattern: {ruleSetName}_p{priority:02d}.txt Response: { "files": [{"filename": ..., "priority": ..., "rowCount": ...}] }
When a DeviceRuleSet spans more than one DeviceRule, all devices sharing the same PRIORITY are included in a single file, each parameter prefixed with the PHYSICAL_DEVICE.deviceName. Provide deviceId or deviceRuleId to restrict the export to a subset of rules.
Persistent trajectories: Trajectory waypoints stored as DeviceConfig/Parameter entries (one Parameter per axis per waypoint, under a dedicated DeviceRuleSet) are exported by this same service alongside regular device configuration — no separate code path needed. The gateway serialises them as Codesys txt-recipe lines that the PLC recipe FB loads from the filesystem and interpolates autonomously. This is the persistent trajectory path, as opposed to export#Trajectory which sends ephemeral bulk data via MQTT for immediate execution by MqttParameterSub. The same distinction is documented in the moqui-device-gateway Camel route configuration: /api/device-config/export handles both device config and persistent trajectories; /api/trajectory/export handles the ephemeral path.
Moqui-side DeviceRequest fields: deviceId = moqui-device-gateway1 routerEnumId = DrrMoquiDeviceGateway requestTypeEnumId = DrtConfigWrite runServiceName = moqui.device.DeviceGatewayServices.export#DeviceConfig brokerUri = gateway REST base URL (e.g. http://gateway-host:8081?apiKey=secret) timeout = HTTP timeout in seconds (optional; defaults to 30)

parameter.name: deviceId
parameter.description: Optional. When set, limits the export to parameters belonging to this device's rules only.

parameter.name: deviceRuleId
parameter.description: Optional. When set, limits the export to a single DeviceRule. Can be combined with deviceId for more specific filtering.

actions.script:
import org.moqui.util.RestClient
def dr = ec.entity.find("moqui.device.DeviceRequest") .condition("requestName", requestName).one() if (!dr) { ec.message.addError("DeviceRequest ${requestName} not found.") return }
String rawUri = dr.brokerUri if (!rawUri) { ec.message.addError("DeviceRequest ${requestName} is missing brokerUri (gateway REST base URL).") return } String gatewayBaseUrl = rawUri String apiKey = null int qIdx = rawUri.indexOf('?') if (qIdx >= 0) { gatewayBaseUrl = rawUri.substring(0, qIdx) rawUri.substring(qIdx + 1).split('&').each { String kv -> String[] parts = kv.split('=', 2) if (parts.length == 2 && 'apiKey' == parts[0].trim()) apiKey = parts[1].trim() } } gatewayBaseUrl = gatewayBaseUrl.replaceAll('/+$', '')
int timeoutSec = dr.timeout != null ? (int) dr.timeout : 30 RestClient rc = new RestClient() .method(RestClient.POST) .uri(gatewayBaseUrl + "/api/device-config/export") .contentType("application/json") .acceptContentType("application/json") .timeout(timeoutSec) if (apiKey) rc.addHeader("X-API-Key", apiKey)
Map<String, Object> payload = [deviceRuleSetId: deviceRuleSetId] if (deviceId) payload.deviceId = deviceId if (deviceRuleId) payload.deviceRuleId = deviceRuleId rc.jsonObject(payload)
RestClient.RestResponse resp = rc.call() resp.checkError()
ec.logger.info("DeviceConfig export completed for ruleSet ${deviceRuleSetId}: ${resp.text()}")

service.description:
Transfers a DeviceContent file (G-Code, CNC program, firmware, etc.) to a device via the moqui-device-gateway process.
Data Model Conventions Moqui-side DeviceRequest fields: deviceId = moqui-device-gateway1 routerEnumId = DrrMoquiDeviceGateway requestTypeEnumId = DrtContentTransfer runServiceName = moqui.device.DeviceGatewayServices.transfer#DeviceContent brokerUri = gateway REST base URL (e.g. http://gateway-host:8081?apiKey=secret) query = gateway-side DeviceRequest.requestName
Gateway-side DeviceRequest fields: requestTypeEnumId = DrtContentTransfer brokerUri = Camel SFTP or file endpoint e.g. sftp:operator@cnc1.factory.local:22/programs file:/mnt/cnc-share/programs
The file is read from DeviceContent.contentLocation via the Moqui resource system and streamed as Base64 JSON to the gateway, which decodes and transfers it via the Camel SFTP/file component to the destination in its brokerUri.

Exports a computed trajectory as a structured JSON payload to the moqui-device-gateway for MQTT publishing (ephemeral bulk data path — counterpart to export#DeviceConfig for persistent recipe data).
Reads ApproximatedFunctionSample + VectorComponent records for the given approximatedFunctionId in sequenceNum order and serialises them as per-axis value arrays. The gateway receives the payload and publishes it to the MQTT topic specified in DeviceRequest.query; MqttParameterSub on the PLC then delivers each key/value pair to JsonToParametersMapper.
Axis names are derived from VectorComponent.dimensionIndex (0 → "J1", …, 5 → "J6"). TrajectoryAxisBinding is intentionally not used here: it belongs to run#RobotArmTrajectoryPlanner as a servo-drive parameter limit verification tool.
JSON payload shape sent to gateway: { "approximatedFunctionId": "100051", "waypointCount": 10, "mqttTopic": "moqui/robot/arm1/trajectory", "axes": { "J1": [0.10, 0.20, …], "J2": [0.00, 0.05, …], … } }
Gateway endpoint: POST /api/trajectory/export
Moqui-side DeviceRequest fields: deviceId = moqui-device-gateway1 routerEnumId = DrrMoquiDeviceGateway brokerUri = gateway REST base URL (e.g. http://gateway-host:8081?apiKey=secret) query = MQTT topic (e.g. moqui/robot/arm1/trajectory)

parameter.name: requestName
parameter.description: Moqui-side DeviceRequest.requestName (routerEnumId=DrrMoquiDeviceGateway) used to resolve the gateway brokerUri and MQTT topic (DeviceRequest.query).

service.description:
Receives inbound error and recovery notifications sent by the moqui-device-gateway process when persistent DB write failures are detected or cleared.
The gateway sends a POST to this endpoint when: - eventType = "inboundError": consecutive DB failures have exceeded gateway.inbound.error.notification.threshold.seconds. - eventType = "inboundRecovered": the first successful DB write after a period of errors.
Configure the gateway to call this service: gateway.inbound.error.notification.uri = http://moqui:8080/rest/s1/org/moqui/device/receiveGatewayNotification
POST body shape: { "eventType": "inboundError" | "inboundRecovered", "routeId": "device-request-consumer-VPL_OPCUA_READ_REQ-0", "requestName": "VPL_OPCUA_READ_REQ", "protocol": "OPC UA", "errorMessage": "Connection refused", // present on inboundError only "firstErrorTime": "2026-05-02T10:00:00Z", "errorDurationSeconds": 63 }
